compare_page.title: California certified point-of-use after a utility PFAS record should stay system-led and proportional
compare_page.lede: This compare page is for California households that already read a seeded utility dossier and now need a certified point-of-use shortlist without flattening Sacramento and East Los Angeles into one state-level buying answer.
compare_page.nextActionTitle: Keep the compare lane tied to the exact California utility dossier
compare_page.nextActionSummary: Use California as a navigation layer, but keep the shortlist anchored to the exact system record, its timing, and the current utility interpretation before comparing certified point-of-use hardware.
comparisonAxes: Claim-level PFAS support for an ingestion-focused route
Whether the product stays proportionate to a California utility dossier
whyThis: California now has two seeded utility dossiers with different route posture.
Households searching within California still need a system-led compare lane rather than a statewide roundup.
This page gives the project a narrower commercial lane than a generic best-filter page.
whatThisDoesNotTellYou: This page does not replace the utility dossier.
It does not turn one California record into a fit for another system.
It does not justify whole-house treatment.
evidenceHighlights: Sacramento and East Los Angeles give the compare lane both a detectable CCR route and a treated-water non-detect dossier.
EPA filter guidance still keeps claim review tied to exact certified products.
A California compare lane is strongest when it stays downstream from the system record.
targetQueries: california pfas filter after utility report
california pfas filter after water notice

sections.title: Why California needs its own post-utility compare lane
sections.summary: A regional compare lane is useful only if it stays downstream from the exact system record instead of pretending California is one PFAS document.
paragraphs: The California cluster now gives the project two distinct public-water entry points with different route posture. That makes a regional compare page useful because it can answer the buying query without erasing the fact that the household still belongs to one exact system.
This page exists to stop the state label from becoming lazy commercial shorthand. The system record still leads.
bullets: The exact utility dossier is the evidence layer.
Certified point-of-use is the commercial layer.

sections.title: What the shortlist should reward
sections.summary: The strongest shortlist keeps maintenance and claim fit visible while blocking whole-house drift.
paragraphs: A California household coming out of a utility dossier usually needs a tighter point-of-use decision, not a broader prestige ladder. That means the page should reward claim scope, installation realism, and annual ownership burden before anything else.
Products that stay proportionate to an ingestion-focused public-water route should remain on top. Bigger and louder hardware should still need separate justification.

sections.title: How this protects both trust and conversion quality
sections.summary: It gives California users a buying step without rewriting the record they just read.
paragraphs: A statewide buying query often hides a much better intent: 'I already saw a utility record and now need a narrower next step.' This page is built for that exact moment.
By holding the system dossier and the point-of-use shortlist in the same frame, the page supports cleaner clicks and a more defensible commercial path.
bullets: It answers a commercial query without skipping interpretation.
It improves click quality by narrowing the path.
It keeps California from collapsing into location-spam commerce.
